Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists AthCCT1 as AT2G32260.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Title and abstract explicitly name Arabidopsis CTP:phosphocholine cytidylyltransferase 1.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number 2.7.7.15 is given anywhere in the paper text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AthCCT1 is explicitly placed in the CCT family and described structurally in Fig. 5.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Discussion explicitly states AthCCT1 has an important role in PC biosynthesis via the CDP-choline pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Paper refers to PC de novo biosynthesis via CDP-choline pathway, matching this subpathway description.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Abstract states CCT1 is considered to catalyze a key regulatory step in primary PC de novo biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Abstract explicitly calls CCT1 a key regulatory step in primary PC de novo biosynthesis.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Enzyme assays quantify CDP-choline production from substrates CTP and phosphocholine, directly supporting transfer to form CDP-choline.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Methods and Fig. 5 legend explicitly state assays used substrates CTP and phosphocholine.

Primary_Product.reason: Fig. 5 legend and enzyme assay methods explicitly state production/quantification of CDP-choline.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: CDP-choline is directly presented as product of AthCCT1 and as intermediate in PC biosynthesis, so immediate precursor connection is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: PC is the final nutrient/metabolic product linked to AthCCT1 in lipidomics.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: PC is described as a phospholipid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper states PC is a major membrane phospholipid involved in membrane function and stress responses.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PC intensity was measured in developing siliques.

Source_Species.reason: AthCCT1 is the Arabidopsis source gene used.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Source gene is from Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: AthCCT1 CDS was obtained from Col-0 plant.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Function was assessed in developing siliques and seedlings/roots, as stated in Results and methods.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Phaseolin, tCUP3, Glycinin, and CaMV 35S are listed in Table 1/methods.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly described and used in the study.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Normal conditions, 200 mM mannitol, and lipid vesicles in biochemical assays are explicitly stated.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results directly show loss reduces PC intensity and stress-responsive root growth; complementation restores phenotype.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These statements are all directly supported by Results figures and text.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results and Fig. 7 support S187 in J segment, reduced lipid-induced helical change, fewer membrane contacts of AI region, and AI region closer to αE helix in S187D simulations.

Omics_Data.reason: Lipidomics platform and absence of public accession are consistent with methods/data availability.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential application is inferable but not explicitly claimed in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show stress-sensitive short roots under mannitol with suppression and no substantial changes in TAG/GL.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SnRK1 is explicitly cited as the kinase mainly phosphorylating AthCCT1 at S187 in prior work referenced by this study.

corrections.reason: No EC number 2.7.7.15 is given anywhere in the paper text.

Regulator_Type.reason: SnRK1 is referred to as mediating phosphorylation of AthCCT1, consistent with being a protein kinase; the name itself denotes SNF1-related protein kinase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SnRK1 is explicitly named, which stands for SNF1-related protein kinase 1.

Source_Species.reason: The study concerns Arabidopsis AthCCT1 and cites previous work on its phosphorylation in Arabidopsis context.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Paper organism is Arabidopsis thaliana, and AthCCT1 phosphorylation by SnRK1 is discussed in that context.

Applied_Species.reason: The applied species in this study is Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: All lines are in Col-0 background.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: SnRK1 is discussed as regulating AthCCT1, which controls PC biosynthesis; thus PC is the relevant downstream product.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: PC is a phospholipid in this study.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper describes PC as a major membrane phospholipid involved in membrane function and stress responses.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results/abstract link S187 phosphorylation state to AthCCT1 enzymatic activity and hence PC biosynthesis regulation.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: AthCCT1 is explicitly the phosphorylation target discussed for SnRK1.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SnRK1 is linked to AthCCT1 through phosphorylation in the Results.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results explicitly state AthCCT1 was mainly phosphorylated by SnRK1 at S187.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Phosphomimetic S187D/E reduced AthCCT1 activity, supporting negative regulation of AthCCT1 enzymatic activity by phosphorylation at this site.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The study did not directly test SnRK1 mutants or manipulate SnRK1 itself; reduced PC biosynthetic capacity and compromised root growth are inferred through AthCCT1 phosphomimetic state, not directly shown for SnRK1.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: SnRK1 is implicated through prior phosphorylation evidence and S187D mimic, but this paper does not directly demonstrate SnRK1 as a condition-relevant decisive regulator of PC levels.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper cites prior phosphorylation evidence and uses phosphomimetic enzyme assays, structural analyses, and S187D complementation in planta.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Mannitol stress and lipid vesicle context are explicitly part of the tests used to infer phosphorylation consequences.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S promoter was used to express the AthCCT1 S187D phosphomimetic mutant in planta.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are stated or directly supported: prior work showed SnRK1 phosphorylation at Ser187; S187D reduced activity/conformational response; S187D failed to rescue short-root phenotype.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects that SnRK1 itself was not directly tested in Results and is included because of its explicit regulatory link to AthCCT1.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Although the study uses Col-0 Arabidopsis, the paper does not explicitly state a SnRK1 source variety.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The downstream product PC was measured in developing siliques.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an extrapolated application and not explicitly proposed in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The statement is inferential; the paper does not directly test SnRK1 perturbation to show tradeoffs.
